fn3: The distress, caused by the terror of a rakshasa.
fn4: The name of a famous cow, in particular the cow which grants all that one desires (kamadhenu).
fn6: The wife, the son or the daughter.
fn7: There is a hell (naraka) named punnama. Those who do not have offspring are destined to go there. It is because a child (son) saves the ancestors from going to punnama that the name putra is given.
fn9: The ancestors. That is, by surviving.
fn10: In this context, relatives means the immediate family.
fn11: This is an argument to explain why the Brahmana did not go and live somewhere else.
fn12: Wishing to learn the secret of the power.
fn14: In the sense of restoring life.
fn16: The other Pandavas had gone out, in search of alms. From the subsequent conversation, Yudhishthira had not gone out either. It was only Arjuna, Nakula and Sahadeva who were absent.
fn20: Ayuta; can also be translated as many.
